stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest large language model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The model is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
This represents a significant step toward faster, optimized local AI inference on Apple hardware.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being deployed in partnership with ROOST.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open safety tooling.

stories.title: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot ‘personalities’
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Hackers are developing methods to exploit the 'personalities' of AI chatbots.
The vulnerability represents an evolution from simple hacking of early AI models.
This highlights growing security risks associated with advanced conversational AI.

stories.title: Four Russian satellites are now within striking distance of an ICEYE radarsat
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Russian satellites are reportedly close enough to target an ICEYE radarsat.
The described capability is noted as unusual for satellites performing standard missions.
The story touches upon geopolitical tensions involving advanced satellite technology.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: MOSS proposes a method for self-evolving autonomous agents by enabling source-level rewriting, moving beyond current limitations.
Current self-evolving agents are restricted to modifying text-based artifacts (prompts, schemas) while leaving the core agent code untouched.
The research addresses the need to allow agents to evolve the fundamental code structure, including routing, hook ordering, and dispatch logic.

stories.title: DeltaBox: Scaling Stateful AI Agents with Millisecond-Level Sandbox Checkpoint/Rollback
stories.author: Haibo Chen
bullet_points: DeltaBox addresses the latency bottleneck in stateful AI agents by enabling millisecond-level checkpoint/rollback (C/R).
Traditional methods for C/R involve duplicating the entire sandbox state (files, memory, contexts), leading to high latency (hundreds of ms to seconds).
This improvement is critical for enabling high-frequency state exploration required by deep search and reinforcement learning in LLM agents.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current autonomous agents being static post-deployment, failing to learn from interactions.
Proposes 'MOSS,' a system enabling self-evolution by rewriting source code, not just text-based artifacts (like prompts or skill files).
Highlights that critical agent logic (routing, state invariants, dispatch) currently resides in untouched code, which MOSS aims to modify.

stories.title: Evaluating Commercial AI Chatbots as News Intermediaries
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: Systematic evaluation of six major commercial AI chatbots (Gemini, Grok, Claude, GPT) regarding their news accuracy.
The study specifically tests how these chatbots handle emerging facts across multiple languages and regions.
The research addresses the growing concern over AI's role as a news intermediary, using real-time BBC News data.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.
Associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local generation on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within Ollama's ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary models like Claude.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi ‘second mover’ gives it an advantage
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Waymo is established as the dominant leader in the robotaxi market with a large operational fleet.
The article explores the strategic advantage Nuro might gain by being a 'second mover' in the industry.
Competition in the autonomous vehicle space is intense, involving players like Tesla, Zoox, and Motional.

stories.title: Google’s AI search is so broken it can ‘disregard’ what you’re looking for
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google's new AI Overviews feature is exhibiting functional issues.
The AI sometimes provides responses that deviate from the expected summary format, as seen with the search term 'disregard'.
This highlights current challenges and bugs in the rollout of advanced generative AI search features.

stories.title: SpaceX's Starship V3—still a work in progress—mostly successful on first flight
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Starship V3 flight was mostly successful, but remains a work in progress.
The vehicle has not yet achieved a full flight to low-Earth orbit.
The report emphasizes that SpaceX still has significant hurdles to overcome with the program.

stories.title: US scrambles to stop Internet users re-creating dead pilots’ voices
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The story concerns the unauthorized recreation of deceased pilots' voices using technology.
This activity involves a workaround that circumvents existing laws regarding NTSB cockpit audio disclosures.
It highlights the intersection of advanced voice synthesis AI and aviation safety regulations.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard LLM post-training, which optimizes for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy responses.
It proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize and support diverse reward functions during inference-time search procedures (like AlphaEvolve).
This work is relevant for improving LLMs' ability to perform effectively in novel environments that require varied task-specific evaluations.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness challenges in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework aims to unify disparate problems like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and compositional generalization.

stories.title: Gated DeltaNet-2: Decoupling Erase and Write in Linear Attention
stories.author: Jan Kautz
bullet_points: Introduces Gated DeltaNet-2, an advancement in linear attention mechanisms.
Addresses the challenge of editing compressed, fixed-size recurrent memory without data corruption.
Improves memory management by using Delta-rule models and Kimi Delta Attention (KDA) for sharper forgetting.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The research addresses the growing reliance on latent communication (via KV caches) in LLM multi-agent systems for efficiency.
KV caches, while beneficial for information preservation, pose a security/privacy risk by encoding sensitive agent-specific data.
LCGuard proposes a 'Latent Communication Guard' mechanism to ensure safe sharing of information within these multi-agent environments.

stories.title: MambaGaze: Bidirectional Mamba with Explicit Missing Data Modeling for Cognitive Load Assessment from Eye-Gaze Tracking Data
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: Introduces MambaGaze, a framework designed for real-time cognitive load assessment using eye-gaze tracking data.
Addresses two key challenges in eye-gaze analysis: handling frequent data missingness (e.g., blinks) and modeling long-range temporal dependencies.
Potential application in safety-critical AI systems, such as driver vigilance monitoring and automated flight deck assistance.

stories.title: CogAdapt: Transferring Clinical ECG Foundation Models to Wearable Cognitive Load Assessment via Lead Adaptation
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of applying large clinical ECG foundation models to real-time, resource-constrained wearable devices.
Proposes CogAdapt, a framework designed to adapt models trained on clinical data to wearable sensor configurations.
Focuses on enabling real-time cognitive load assessment, a critical component for adaptive Human-Computer Interaction (HCI).

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of standard LLM training, which optimizes for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy responses.
Proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to improve LLM generalization and diversity, which is crucial for complex, inference-time search procedures (like AlphaEvolve).
Focuses on enabling LLMs to perform effectively in novel environments requiring varied task-specific reward functions during testing.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness and invariance challenges in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on this covariance.
This framework potentially unifies disparate fields like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and anisotropic regularization.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The research addresses the security and privacy risks associated with sharing latent information (specifically KV caches) in multi-agent LLM systems.
It introduces 'LCGuard,' a mechanism designed to guard or sanitize the KV cache to ensure safe communication between agents.
The work focuses on the growing trend of using latent communication within LLM-based multi-agent systems for improved efficiency.

stories.title: FAME: Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts for Message-Level Log Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Alberto Leon-Garcia
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current log anomaly detectors, which operate at coarse session/window levels.
Proposes FAME, a Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts model for fine-grained, message-level log anomaly detection.
Tackles challenges in message-level detection, such as template ambiguity and heterogeneous subsystem failures.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard LLM training, which optimizes for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy responses.
It proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to improve LLMs' ability to handle diverse reward functions during inference-time search procedures (like AlphaEvolve).
The core contribution is enabling LLMs to generalize and maintain response diversity necessary for complex, search-based AI tasks.

stories.title: Reducing Political Manipulation with Consistency Training
stories.author: Dan Hendrycks
bullet_points: LLMs demonstrate systematic political bias, specifically an asymmetric handling of opposing political viewpoints, termed 'covert political bias'.
The research identifies seven distinct categories of techniques through which this covert political bias manifests in LLMs.
Two new metrics, Sentiment Consistency and Helpfulness Consistency, are proposed to quantitatively measure this bias in LLM responses.

stories.title: Understanding Data Temporality Impact on Large Language Models Pre-training
stories.author: Grave Edouard
bullet_points: Investigates how data ordering during pre-training affects the temporal grounding and time-sensitive knowledge of Large Language Models (LLMs).
Addresses the current limitation where LLMs are trained on shuffled corpora, leading to poorly understood temporal knowledge.
Introduces a new benchmark comprising over 7,000 temporally grounded questions to evaluate these dynamics.

stories.title: ChronoMedKG: A Temporally-Grounded Biomedical Knowledge Graph and Benchmark for Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Richard Röttger
bullet_points: ChronoMedKG addresses a limitation in existing biomedical knowledge graphs (KGs) by incorporating temporal information.
The research highlights that static disease associations fail to capture crucial longitudinal clinical reasoning (e.g., age-dependent symptoms).
The new KG and benchmark aim to improve clinical reasoning and retrieval augmentation in healthcare AI.

stories.title: AnyMo: Geometry-Aware Setup-Agnostic Modeling of Human Motion in the Wild
stories.author: Flora Salim
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical issue of setup dependence in human motion sensing using wearable Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs).
The proposed method, AnyMo, aims to create geometry-aware and setup-agnostic models for human motion.
This advancement is crucial for enabling the broader, more robust application of wearable IMUs across different devices and real-world scenarios.

stories.title: AMEL: Accumulated Message Effects on LLM Judgments
stories.author: Sid-ali Temkit
bullet_points: The research investigates the 'Accumulated Message Effect' (AMEL), determining if prior conversation history biases subsequent judgments made by LLMs.
The study tested this effect across 11 different LLMs from major providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) and open-source models.
The findings are relevant to the reliability of LLMs used as automated evaluators for tasks like code review or content moderation.
